text.title: AT Test Workflows and Agentic Pipeline
text.summary: Tiered test workflows from 15-minute smoke to 4-hour comprehensive, plus an agentic pipeline using Playwright, Guidepup, and human sign-off.

rule.statement: Run a 15-minute smoke test on every PR; reserve the comprehensive 2-4 hour pass for release candidates and major feature launches.
rule.rationale: Full AT testing is expensive; catching regressions early on targeted flows prevents compound debt without blocking velocity.

example.code:
1. Keyboard pass (5 min) - Tab through entire page - All interactive elements reachable? - Focus indicator always visible? - No keyboard traps?
2. Screen reader spot check (5 min) - Navigate headings (H key in NVDA) - Navigate landmarks (D key in NVDA) - Try one form field — label announced?
3. Automated scan (5 min) - Run axe DevTools - Zero critical violations?

1. Automated scan (30 min): axe + WAVE + Lighthouse; log all issues. 2. Keyboard navigation (1 hr): full Tab pass; all interactions; custom components. 3. Screen reader (1-2 hr): NVDA+Firefox (or JAWS+Chrome); headings, landmarks, all forms, dynamic content, custom widgets. 4. Mobile SR (30 min): VoiceOver on iOS Safari; TalkBack on Android Chrome; test gestures; test forms.

section.title: Agentic Pipeline

rule.statement: Drive AT testing as a tiered chain: Playwright+axe for tree coverage, Guidepup for real-speech critical flows, human tester for final sign-off on custom widgets.
rule.rationale: Automation keeps regressions cheap; it cannot replace an expert AT user for nuanced judgment on custom components, live regions, and mobile gestures.

section.reference: Real-speech Playwright + Guidepup test for NVDA form-error announcement
Gherkin template for AT test cases anchored to WCAG SC

list.item: Playwright agent runs axe-core for tree-level coverage on every PR.
Guidepup agent drives NVDA/VoiceOver for curated critical flows (form submit, modal, SR navigation).
Human AT user signs off on custom widgets, live-region semantics, mobile gesture coverage.

antipattern.description: Asserting exact SR speech phrases such as "screen reader will say 'invalid'".
antipattern.reason: Over-specifies phrasing; NVDA and JAWS render the same ARIA differently. Assert on intent (error announced within 1s) not exact string.

antipattern.description: Closing an a11y bug without linking to the WCAG criterion and pre/post screenshot.
antipattern.reason: Reviewers cannot audit drift; regressions are silently reintroduced.

rule.statement: Treat focus restoration after modal close as its own checklist item; it is the most common AT regression.
rule.rationale: Frameworks re-render on modal close and reset focus to document.body; must explicitly return focus to the trigger element.
